title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new premium electric bicycle they’ve tried.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally took the 'Volt-Rider' for a spin. That carbon frame makes it incredibly light for an e-bike. || Woman: [1]I found the integrated touch-screen on the handlebars to be a bit of a distraction[/1], though. I kept glancing down at the stats instead of watching the road. || Man: I see your point. It’s a bit too much information at once. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the smoothness of the motor assistance[/2]. It didn't feel like it was jerking forward every time I started pedalling. || Man: I agree. It’s very intuitive. Though I’m not sure I can justify the five-thousand-pound price tag. || Woman: It’s definitely a luxury item, but for a serious commuter, it might be worth the investment.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally took the 'Volt-Rider' for a spin. That carbon frame makes it incredibly light for an e-bike.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the integrated touch-screen on the handlebars to be a bit of a distraction, though. I kept glancing down at the stats instead of watching the road.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. It’s a bit too much information at once.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the smoothness of the motor assistance. It didn't feel like it was jerking forward every time I started pedalling.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s very intuitive. Though I’m not sure I can justify the five-thousand-pound price tag.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s definitely a luxury item, but for a serious commuter, it might be worth the investment.

questions.question: What is the woman's main concern about the bicycle's design?
questions.explanation: She finds the screen a 'distraction' that stops her from 'watching the road' [1].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the bike's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'smoothness' and 'intuitive' nature of the motor assistance [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new 'well-being' app provided by their employer.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you downloaded the 'Mind-Check' app yet? I’ve been using the guided meditation during my lunch break. || Woman: [3]I suspect the company is using it to collect data on employee stress levels[/3] rather than out of any real concern for our health. || Man: That's a bit cynical! It’s meant to be anonymous. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the sleep-tracking feature requires you to have your phone right next to your pillow[/4], which actually makes it harder for me to switch off. || Man: I agree. It’s a bit ironic that a wellness app keeps you glued to your screen. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a well-meaning idea, but the execution feels a bit counter-productive in a high-pressure office environment.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you downloaded the 'Mind-Check' app yet? I’ve been using the guided meditation during my lunch break.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the company is using it to collect data on employee stress levels rather than out of any real concern for our health.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a bit cynical! It’s meant to be anonymous.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the sleep-tracking feature requires you to have your phone right next to your pillow, which actually makes it harder for me to switch off.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a bit ironic that a wellness app keeps you glued to your screen.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a well-meaning idea, but the execution feels a bit counter-productive in a high-pressure office environment.

questions.question: The woman believes the app's primary purpose is

options.A: to reduce the cost of healthcare premiums.
options.C: to increase the speed of daily tasks.

questions.explanation: She thinks it's about 'collecting data on employee stress levels' [3].

questions.question: What paradox do both speakers identify regarding the app?

options.A: It is too expensive for a free service.
options.B: It encourages the habits it aims to reduce.
options.C: It is difficult to use for non-technical staff.

questions.explanation: They agree it's ironic that a 'wellness' app requires more screen time and prevents switching off [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently renovated public library.
extracts.audio_script: Man: The new library is stunning, isn't it? All that glass and natural light. || Woman: [5]I found the lack of comfortable seating to be a bit of a mistake[/5], though. All the chairs are very stylish but incredibly hard to sit in for more than ten minutes. || Man: I see your point. It’s a bit 'form over function'. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the expanded digital archive, though[/6]. Being able to access those old local newspapers on a tablet is brilliant. || Man: I agree. The technology they’ve brought in is definitely a step up. || Woman: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to bring a cushion next time I want to do some proper research.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The new library is stunning, isn't it? All that glass and natural light.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the lack of comfortable seating to be a bit of a mistake, though. All the chairs are very stylish but incredibly hard to sit in for more than ten minutes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It’s a bit 'form over function'.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the expanded digital archive, though. Being able to access those old local newspapers on a tablet is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. The technology they’ve brought in is definitely a step up.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I’ll just make sure to bring a cushion next time I want to do some proper research.

questions.question: What did the woman dislike about her visit to the library?

options.A: the noise level in the study areas
options.B: the poor quality of the furniture
options.C: the limited range of physical books

questions.explanation: She finds the chairs 'incredibly hard' and lacking in comfort [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the library's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'digital archive' and the 'step up' in technology [6].
